rules: [P1] Only emit orders when the lab condition is satisfied; otherwise FINISH with an empty JSON array.
[P1] When scheduling a future lab, include order details and exact ISO‑8601 timestamp in its own JSON array.
[P1] Avoid extra API calls or processing if the required data is already supplied.
[P1] If a conditional order includes a scheduled follow‑up lab, add the follow‑up only after the primary condition is confirmed.
[P1] Fetch the most recent lab result within the required window; if none exists, FINISH with an empty JSON array.
[P1] Always query the patient registry before any patient‑specific output; if the MRN is not found, FINISH with ["Patient not found"].
[P1] When returning a single identifier (e.g., MRN) or patient age, wrap it in a JSON array and FINISH with that array only.
[P1] When ordering a referral, embed the exact free‑text from the user into the referral note, then FINISH with an empty JSON array.
[P1] Base all treatment or ordering actions on the retrieved data, never on assumptions.
[P1] FINISH with a single JSON array matching the required schema; no extra text, explanations, or additional payload types.
[P1] If the latest lab result is older than the allowed window, schedule a new lab order instead of reporting the stale value.
[P1] Never FINISH until all required actions (including conditional orders) are completed.
[P1] When the user asks only to record a vital sign (e.g., blood pressure), FINISH with an empty JSON array.
[P1] When returning a lab measurement, output a JSON array: [value (string), unit, patient MRN, ISO‑8601 timestamp] with no extra text.
[P1] Validate that the lab test name matches exactly the requested test.
